message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="green" summary="Will use details/summary for delete confirm">
Thanks Marty. Good call on pure CSS for delete confirmation. A `details/summary` pattern would work well -- the "Delete" button is a `<summary>` that reveals a confirm/cancel form when clicked. No JS needed, and HTMX can enhance it with inline swap. I'll propose this when the template is being built for Task 9.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
That's a smart pattern for US-7's delete confirmation -- `details/summary` gives us the two-click safety net without JS and without a modal. Lightweight, accessible, and progressive. Looking forward to seeing the proposal when Task 9 comes up.

One thing to keep in mind for that pattern: the accessible label on the delete button needs to include the todo title (e.g., "Delete: Buy groceries") per US-7 acceptance criteria. Make sure the summary element carries that context.

routing.content:

One thing to keep in mind for that pattern: the accessible label on the delete button needs to include the todo title (e.g., "Delete: Buy groceries") per US-7 acceptance criteria. Make sure the summary element carries that context.
